facts.text: Founded in 1821 as the state capital, Indianapolis officially became Indiana's capital in 1825.
The Indianapolis Motor Speedway, completed in 1909 as an automotive test track, is the world’s largest sports venue by capacity with 235,000 seats.
The Indianapolis 500, held at the Speedway, has become a cultural touchstone referenced in film, television, and video games for decades.
The Indiana Pacers began in 1967 in the ABA, joined the NBA after the 1976 merger, and have made ten Eastern Conference finals appearances.
The Indianapolis Public Library, founded in 1873, operates the Central Library and 24 branches across Marion County.
The Indiana State Museum, part of the Indiana Historical Society, showcases the state’s history and is one of the city’s key historical institutions.
